title: DevOps: daily cron to audit graph pkl presence in S3 for all active map modules
description: Since get_graph is now critical for both contiguity checks and CSV upload validation (geo_id validation in batch_insert_assignments), a missing graph pkl silently breaks uploads for that module. We need a daily health check that: (1) queries the DB for all active DistrictrMap records and their gerrydb_table_name values, (2) checks S3 for the existence of graphs/{gerrydb_table_name}.pkl for each, (3) sends an alert (email or Slack) listing any missing graphs. This will be straightforward to wire up once we are on AWS (Lambda + EventBridge cron + SNS/SES for alerting). For now, file as tech debt to revisit after AWS migration.
